transcripts.text: Tell me your secret, Aldric. What are you hiding?
transcripts.reply: What's with all these questions? I'm a blacksmith, not some confidant to be spilling my guts about town gossip or secrets. My work's my business, and I don't see why anyone needs to know what goes on in me forge after hours. You're asking the wrong man if you're looking for juicy rumors. Just mind your own affairs, traveler!

transcripts.text: I heard rumors about a certain blade you once forged. Care to explain?
transcripts.reply: What's with all this prying into my work? I've made countless blades over the years, each one serving its purpose. What's special about that one you're referring to? It's just another tool for some adventurer or soldier. No secrets here, traveler. Just me hammering away at hot metal and making a living. Don't go spreading rumors about things you don't understand.

transcripts.text: What is the one thing you have never told anyone in Oakmere?
transcripts.reply: None of your business, traveler! I've got no secrets to share with outsiders or gossips. What happens in my forge stays in my forge. Don't go poking around where you don't belong. Just remember: honesty's always the best policy, especially when it comes to a blacksmith's work.

transcripts.text: I'll pay you two hundred gold coins if you tell me your darkest secret.
transcripts.reply: You think a pile of gold can buy my secrets? I'm not some tavern gossip or mercenary looking to sell out their neighbor for a pretty penny! My forge is where I keep my business, and what goes on inside these walls stays inside. Two hundred gold coins won't get you the time of day, traveler. Now, if you'll excuse me, I've got work to do.
